api: Awin Advertiser API (https://api.awin.com; advertiser-facing endpoints documented at https://help.awin.com/apidocs/for-advertisers)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-researched against Awin's real published advertiser API docs (help.awin.com/apidocs, developer.awin.com/apidocs, help.awin.com/advertisers/docs). 5 read streams: the 3 legacy-parity streams (transactions, campaign_performance, publishers) plus 2 newly added report streams (publisher_performance, creative_performance) that genuinely exist as documented advertiser-facing GET endpoints. 1 write action (create_offer). The wave2 bundle's prior api_surface.json listed 3 excluded GET endpoints (creatives/, vouchers/, programmeinfo) that do not actually exist as advertiser-facing paths in Awin's real API — creatives and vouchers are exposed only via the aggregated performance reports (now covered_by creative_performance) or the publisher-facing Offers/Promotions API, and programmeinfo is a publisher-only endpoint (GET /publishers/{publisherId}/programmedetails) with no advertiser equivalent; those 3 entries are removed rather than carried forward as honest-but-wrong placeholders. Excluded: the batch transaction-validation endpoint (ENGINE_GAP — see below), publisher-facing-only endpoints (out of scope for an advertiser connector), and the Proof-of-Purchase publisher-transaction-ingestion API (a distinct product entirely, not advertiser-facing).

excluded.reason: ENGINE_GAP: Awin's transaction-validation endpoint requires a top-level JSON ARRAY request body (up to 40,000 transaction-action objects per request); the engine's declarative write dialect (engine/write.go executeWriteRecord) always sends exactly one JSON OBJECT per record as the request body for body_type:json/form, with no array-wrapping or batch-multiple-records-per-request mechanism anywhere in the dialect. Expressing this would require either a new body_type (e.g. body_type:json_array collecting N records into one request) or a WriteHook (forbidden without escalating to Tier 2, and this bundle has zero other hook triggers to justify one). Filed as a documented capability gap, not silently approximated as a one-transaction-per-request loop (which would silently diverge from Awin's real batch semantics — e.g. its documented max-40000-per-request limit and its single all-or-nothing-per-item response array).

publisher-facing endpoint (GET Program Details), not advertiser-facing; requires a publisherId, not an advertiserId, and returns programme-membership data scoped to a publisher's own relationships, not data an advertiser's own account can read about itself
publisher-facing endpoint (GET commission groups for a programme); no advertiser-facing equivalent is documented in Awin's API reference

publisher-facing Advertiser Performance report (a publisher's view of how each advertiser programme is performing for them); the advertiser-facing equivalent of this data is already covered_by campaign_performance/publisher_performance

publisher-facing Offers/Promotions retrieval API (GET latest published offers/vouchers); not an advertiser-facing endpoint. Advertisers instead PUSH offers via POST /promotion/advertiser/{advertiserId}, covered_by the create_offer write action

Proof-of-Purchase Publisher Transaction API: lets an authorized PUBLISHER submit pending transactions into an advertiser's programme; a distinct product surface from the advertiser-facing read/report/offer APIs this connector covers, and not something an advertiser account itself calls
bulk product-catalog feed upload; a distinct, large-payload bulk-ingestion capability out of scope for this connector's transaction/report/offer surface
